era: 1987-present. Grunge/Metal. E=9. Capitol Hill/Seattle. Columbia Records. Dark harmonics, Layne Staley's voice, metal foundation beneath grunge surface. Second Seattle band in the summit sequence, post-Soundgarden.
members: Layne Staley (vocals, born August 22 1967 Kirkland WA, died April 5 2002 Seattle WA, age 34)
Jerry Cantrell (guitar/vocals, born March 18 1966 Tacoma WA)
Sean Kinney (drums, born May 27 1966 Seattle WA)
key_albums: Facelift (1990, Columbia) -- debut; We Die Young, Man in the Box, Sea of Sorrow
Dirt (1992, Columbia) -- masterwork; Rooster, Down in a Hole, Would?, Angry Chair; Staley's addiction documented
Alice in Chains EP (1992, Columbia) -- Angry Chair acoustic; bridge between albums
Jar of Flies EP (1993, Columbia) -- acoustic; No Excuses; first acoustic EP to debut at #1
Alice in Chains (1995, Columbia) -- self-titled; Grind, Over Now, Frogs; last full album with Staley
Black Gives Way to Blue (2009, Virgin) -- reunion debut with DuVall; tribute to Staley
The Devil Put Dinosaurs Here (2013, Virgin) -- sophomore with DuVall

theory_nodes.concept: DUAL VOCALS as primary signature. Cantrell and Staley harmonized in minor thirds and dissonant intervals not standard in rock -- parallel fifths, tritones as color tones, dissonant harmonies held without resolution. The Cantrell-Staley dual vocal is the most harmonically distinctive element in the grunge canon. No other grunge band achieved this level of vocal dissonance as central aesthetic.
theory_nodes.description: The Cantrell-Staley dual vocal as grunge's most harmonically sophisticated vocal element.

theory_nodes.concept: E=9 CALIBRATION: Metal foundation (drop-D, heavy riffing, palm muting) beneath grunge surface (Seattle production, lyrical darkness, Sub Pop cultural adjacency) plus the Layne Staley vocal weight (E=9 vocal presence alone). Alice in Chains operates at the metal edge of grunge -- heaviest alongside Soundgarden, distinct in harmonic approach. Dirt (1992) alone calibrates E=9.
theory_nodes.description: E=9 from the metal-grunge intersection and the irreplaceable Staley vocal weight.

theory_nodes.concept: Layne Staley (August 22 1967 - April 5 2002). Born Kirkland WA. Voice: lower register, harmonic resonance in mid-range that carries across full-band recording. Control without effort appearing forced. Addiction documented in Dirt (1992) and Alice in Chains (1995) with unflinching precision. Died April 5 2002 -- same date as Cobain's death eight years prior (April 5 1994). The April 5 thread.
theory_nodes.description: Staley's biography and the April 5 thread documented with factual respect.

theory_nodes.concept: Dirt (1992): the definitive Alice in Chains document. Rooster: Cantrell's Vietnam veteran father (Jerry David Cantrell Sr.); specific anti-war statement through personal portrait. Down in a Hole: depression as geology, interiority mapped as spatial depth. Would?: Andrew Wood memorial, Mother Love Bone connection (degrees 296, 305). Angry Chair: Staley's addiction as interior monologue. The album is a complete emotional document.
theory_nodes.description: Dirt as the definitive AIC document and its track-by-track thematic architecture.

theory_nodes.concept: APRIL 5 THREAD: Kurt Cobain died April 5 1994; Layne Staley died April 5 2002. Eight years apart, same date. Both from the grunge generation, both Seattle, both voices of irreplaceable character. The April 5 date is the engine's most significant biographical coincidence. The date marks the losses of grunge's two most artistically irreplaceable voices.
theory_nodes.description: April 5 thread: Cobain (1994) and Staley (2002) both died on the same date, eight years apart.

theory_nodes.concept: Jerry Cantrell's guitar: drop-D tuning standard for the era, but the harmonic approach exceeds the tuning. Lydian inflections in clean passages; tritone substitutions in heavy sections; the guitar harmonics were built to interact with Staley's voice specifically. After Staley's death, Cantrell maintained the Alice in Chains identity through composition rather than imitation.
theory_nodes.description: Cantrell's guitar approach as built for the Staley vocal and its continuation post-2002.

cross_references.relationship: Consecutive E=9 entries. Both at the metal edge of grunge. Soundgarden: University District, 1984. Alice in Chains: Capitol Hill/Seattle, 1987. Both Columbia-era major label grunge bands with E=9 energy. Post-Summit sequence begins with AIC.
Would? (Dirt, 1992) is a direct tribute to Andrew Wood. The Mother Love Bone connection runs through Alice in Chains via this track. Wood's death (March 1990) is documented in AIC's work.
April 5 thread: Cobain died April 5 1994; Staley died April 5 2002. Same date, eight years apart. The two most artistically irreplaceable voices of grunge lost on the same calendar date.
Mad Season (1995, Layne Staley + Mark Lanegan adjacent). Side project documents Staley's voice outside the AIC framework. May enter engine in future degrees.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=9 + E=9 = E=18. POST-SUMMIT FIRST DEGREE. Alice in Chains E=9 (metal harmonics, Staley's weight) paired with Cougar-Wolf territorial overlap E=9 (two apex predators at contested boundary). Both about territorial assertion at the edge of collision -- AIC's dark harmonics at the edge of metal and grunge, the Cougar-Wolf encounter at the edge of two apex predator territories.
